subject: Chemistry
solution:
Formula of ppm is-

ppm=weight of soluteweight of solution×106

ppm=10.3×10-31030×106 =10

PPM is a concentration term and its full form is "parts per million" and it also can be expressed as milligrams per liter (mg/L). This measurement is the mass of a chemical or contaminate per unit volume of water.

subject: Chemistry
solution: On heating concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in water decreases. So boiling water and water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. Polluted water also having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. So water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is having maximum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration.

in the atmosphere, it is depleted by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s.

solution:
In presence of sunlight CFC’s molecule divides & release chlorine free radical, which react with ozone give chlorine monoxide radical (ClO) and oxygen

CF2Cl2g⟶UVC˙lg+C˙F2Clg

Cl*(g)+O3(g)⟶ClO*(g)+O2(g)

ClO∙(g)+O(g)⟶Cl'(g)+O2(g)

subject: Chemistry
solution: Based on fact layer of atmosphere between 10 km to 50 km above sea level is called as stratosphere.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane (DDT) is an insecticide used in agriculture. Dieldrin a decades-old chlorocarbon insecticide that has long been banned from use in most of the world. It was introduced in 1948 by the now-defunct J. Organophosphates are a group of human-made chemicals that poison insects and mammals. Organophosphates are the most widely used insecticides today. They are used in agriculture, the home, gardens, and veterinary practice.

These days, the pesticide industry has shifted its attention to herbicides such as sodium chlorate (NaClO3), sodium arsenite (Na3AsO3 ) and many others.

options: Photochemical smog and ozone layer depletion

subject: Chemistry
options: It is a mixture of smoke, fog and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>.
It results from reaction of unsaturated hydrocarbons.
solution:
Photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate.

Photochemical smog results from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydro carbons and nitrogen oxide.

The common components of photochemical smog are ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN).

It is oxidising smog.

solution:

H2O2 is used as bleaching agent in laundry.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Both Statement I and Statement II are false

Reducing smog is a mixture of smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide. Tropospheric pollutants such as hydrocarbon and nitrogen oxide contribute to the formation of photochemical smog.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Dissolved oxygen (DO) in cold water can reach a concentration upto $10 \mathrm{ppm}$.

subject: Chemistry
solution: DDT(dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) and Aldrin are two synthetic pesticides that were widely used for insect control. Pesticides are groups of chemicals used for destruction of insects, weeds, fungi, bacteria etc. They are generally called insecticides, fungicides, bactericides, herbicides and rodenticides.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
F- ion concentration above 2 ppm causes brown mottling of teeth. At the same time, excess fluoride (over 10 ppm) causes harmful effect to bones and teeth

Lead: Drinking water gets contaminated with lead when lead pipes are used for transportation of water. The prescribed upper limit concentration of lead in drinking water is about 50 ppb. Lead can damage kidney, liver, reproductive system etc.

Sulphate: Excessive sulphate >500 ppm in drinking water causes laxative effect, otherwise at moderate levels it is harmless.

Nitrate: The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methaemoglobinaemia ('blue baby' syndrome).

subject: Chemistry
options: Polluted water has BOD value higher than $10 \mathrm{ppm}$
Aerobic bacteria decreases the BOD value
Anaerobic bacteria increases the BOD value
Clean water has BOD value higher than 10 ppm
solution: Clean water has BOD value less than $5 \mathrm{ppm}$. Polluted water has BOD value higher than 10 ppm.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide (NO2). This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g)→hvNO (g)+O (g)  A B-----(i)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g)+O2 (g)⇌O3 (g)B   C-----(ii)

The ozone formed in the above reaction (ii) reacts rapidly with the NO (g) formed in reaction (i) to regenerate NO2 . NO2 is a brown gas and at sufficiently high levels can contribute to haze.

NO (g)+O3 (g)→NO2 (g)+A C   O2 (g) ----(iii)

So, the A, B and C are NO, O and O3 respectively.

subject: Chemistry
solution: High concentration of $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ leads to stiffness of flower buds.

subject: Chemistry
options: for sustaining life in a water body.
by bacteria to break-down organic waste in a certain volume of a water sample.

for the photochemical breakdown of waste present in <math><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> volume of a water body
by anaerobic bacteria to breakdown. inorganic waste present in a water body.
solution: The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical oxygen Demand (BOD).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A-Sulphate - III (Laxative effect)

Sulfates are a large part of the haze pollution that irritates the lungs and affects your lung health.

B-Fluoride - II (Bending of bones)

Fluoride is a neurotoxin which, in high doses, can be harmful. Excessive exposure can lead to tooth discoloration and bone problems.

C-Nictoine - I (pesticides)

Nicotine has been used as a natural insecticide and is the archetype for the large range of synthetic neonicotinoid insecticides.

D-Sodium Arsinite - IV (herbicide)

Inorganic arsenicals (arsenic trioxide, sodium arsenite and lead arsenate) have been used as rodenticides and herbicides.

solution:
The presence of ozone in troposphere generates photochemical smog.

Ozone acts as an important infrared absorber (greenhouse gas), particularly in the upper troposphere, and also is an absorber of solar ultraviolet (UV) radiation.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Smog is derived from smoke + fog, this is most common example of air pollution, photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate also known as oxidizing smog.
Unburnt hydrocarbons + Nitric oxide
NO2g→hνNOg+Og
O2g+Og→O3g
CH4+2O3→3CH2OFormaldehyde+3H2O
So the common component of photochemical smog are Ozone, No, acrolein, PAN, formaldehyde.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ozone in the stratosphere is a product of UV radiations acting on dioxygen O2 molecules.

O2( g)⟶UVO(g)+O(g)

O(g)+O2( g)⇌UVO3( g)

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Acid rain is a by product of a variety of human activities that emit the oxides of sulphur and nitrogen in the atmosphere. As mentioned earlier, burning of fossil fuels (which contain sulphur and nitrogenous matter) such as coal and oil in power stations and furnaces or petrol and diesel in motor engines produce sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxides. SO2 and NO2 after oxidation and reaction with water are major contributors to acid rain, because polluted air usually contains particulate matter that catalyse the oxidation.

2SO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→2H2SO4aq

4NO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→4HNO3aq

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The gas CH4 evolved due to anaerobic degradation of vegetation, which causes global warming and cancer.

Anaerobic degradation means the breakdown in the absence of oxygen. During anaerobic degradation of vegetation, CH4 is produced, which is responsible for global warming as it absorbs sun’s heat. It is also known to cause cancer.
